pi_items.source: Docker Compose volume mount in docker-compose.yml (/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web:/config) and jitsi/web container image behavior verified via official Jitsi docker-jitsi-meet documentation
Official Jitsi Meet config.js repository showing 'chat' as documented toolbar button: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/config.js
Official Jitsi Meet config.js repository showing 'desktop' as documented toolbar button for screen sharing: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/config.js
Official Jitsi Meet config.js repository showing 'invite' as documented toolbar button: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/config.js
Official Jitsi Meet config.js repository showing 'embedmeeting' as documented toolbar button: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/config.js

Docker Compose v2 uses hyphenated naming format ([project]-[service]-[instance]). With docker-compose-v2 (install_jitsi.sh line 35) running from /home/ga/jitsi directory, service named 'web' generates 'jitsi-web-1'. Verified via Docker Compose documentation: https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/ and Docker issue discussion showing v2 naming convention

Jitsi Meet allows arbitrary room names as meeting identifiers. 'VisitationUnit1' is a valid room name format for this task scenario. Verified by Jitsi architecture where room names are arbitrary XMPP MUC identifiers.

privileged_info_summary: The task is to harden a Jitsi Meet interface for a correctional visitation kiosk by removing four specific toolbar buttons: 'chat', 'desktop' (screen share), 'invite', and 'embedmeeting' from the TOOLBAR_BUTTONS array in /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/interface_config.js. These are all documented, standard Jitsi Meet toolbar buttons. The Jitsi web container (running as 'jitsi-web-1' in Docker Compose v2) must be restarted to apply the configuration changes. Verification involves joining the meeting room 'VisitationUnit1' and confirming these buttons are no longer visible in the interface.
